title_en: China’s semiconductor industry is racing to catch the West’s
title_zh: 中国半导体产业正加速追赶西方
rubric_en: But it is proving easier to design chips than to make them
rubric_zh: 但事实证明，设计芯片比制造芯片更容易

paragraphs.en: When last month the chip industry’s elite descended on Taipei for Computex, an annual trade show, two topics dominated discussions. The first was artificial intelligence, which has turned semiconductors into one of the world’s most profitable industries. The second was China. Its chipmakers were absent from the event, but their shadow was long.
paragraphs.zh: 上个月，当芯片行业的精英们为参加一年一度的台北电子展（Computex）而抵达台北时，讨论中被两大话题主导。第一是人工智能，它已经使半导体成为全球最赚钱的行业之一。第二是中国。虽然中国芯片制造商缺席了这场盛会，但其影响无处不在。

paragraphs.en: The fascination with China is easy to explain. A week before Computex, Huawei, the country’s tech-hardware champion, unveiled a technique that stacks layers of circuits onto a chip to get better performance without relying on the latest Western manufacturing tools. Investors are also signalling confidence. The share prices of semiconductor firms on Hong Kong’s stock market has risen by 140% over the past year, beating a comparable American index. Alibaba and Baidu, two Chinese internet giants, intend to spin off their chip-design businesses. CXMT, a maker of memory chips, is preparing a public listing.
paragraphs.zh: 人们对中国的关注并不难理解。就在 Computex 前一周，华为这家中国的科技硬件龙头推出了一项技术，可在芯片上叠加多层电路，在不依赖最新西方制造设备的情况下提升性能。投资者的信号也在传递信心。香港股市中的半导体公司股价过去一年上涨了140%，超过了可比的美国指数。阿里巴巴和百度这两家中国互联网巨头都计划剥离各自的芯片设计业务。存储芯片制造商长鑫存储（CXMT）正在筹备上市。

paragraphs.en: China’s potential for self-sufficiency in chipmaking is one of the industry’s biggest questions. The answer matters because “compute”—the processors and memory that train and run AI models—has become the currency of technological power. At the end of 2025 China had only around one-seventh of the compute capacity of America. Closing the gap requires mastering two very different activities: designing chips and manufacturing them. China has made remarkable progress in the first. In the second, it has much further to go.
paragraphs.zh: 中国在芯片制造上的自给自足潜力是业界最大的疑问之一。答案至关重要，因为“算力”——用于训练和运行AI模型的处理器与内存——已经成为科技实力的核心货币。到2025年底，中国的算力仅约为美国的七分之一。缩小这一差距需要掌握两项截然不同的能力：芯片设计和芯片制造。中国在第一方面已取得了显著进展；在第二方面，仍有很长的路要走。

paragraphs.en: China used to be miles behind in chip design. Until 2023 Nvidia, which dominates the market for AI chips, met around 90% of China’s demand. Export controls changed that. Since America restricted sales of advanced chips to China in October 2022, Uncle Sam’s policy has oscillated. But China’s response has been steady: regulators have consistently steered domestic firms away from foreign chips, even when they are legally obtainable.
paragraphs.zh: 中国过去在芯片设计方面远远落后。直到2023年，主导AI芯片市场的英伟达曾满足了中国约90%的需求。出口管制改变了这一局面。自2022年10月美国开始限制向中国出售高端芯片以来，美国政策一直在反复摆动，但中国的应对始终保持稳定：监管层持续引导国内企业远离外国产品，即使这些芯片在法律上可以购买。

paragraphs.en: The result has been a protected home market. Alibaba and Baidu are designing processors in-house, while upstarts such as Cambricon have flourished with limited competition. Domestic designers are projected to capture around four-fifths of spending in China on AI chips this year (see chart 1). Qingyuan Lin of Bernstein, a broker, jokes that America has done more to strengthen China’s semiconductor industry than the country’s own government.
paragraphs.zh: 其结果是形成了一个受到保护的本土市场。阿里巴巴和百度都在内部设计处理器，而像寒武纪（Cambricon）这样的新创公司在竞争有限的环境中蓬勃发展。预计今年中国在 AI 芯片上的支出中，国内设计商将占到约五到四成（见图 1）。伯恩斯坦（Bernstein）的一位经纪人林清源（Qingyuan Lin）开玩笑说，美国对中国半导体产业的推动，比中国自己政府的推动更大。

paragraphs.en: China’s market is big enough to sustain an industry. Local cloud giants are expected to spend more than $400bn on AI infrastructure over the next three years, much of it on compute. The government is said to be planning another $295bn of spending on data centres over five years.
paragraphs.zh: 中国市场足够大，能够支撑一个产业。当地的云计算巨头预计在未来三年将花费超过 4000 亿美元用于 AI 基础设施，其中很大一部分用于算力。传闻说，政府正计划在未来五年再投入 2950 亿美元用于建设数据中心。

paragraphs.en: China has plenty of people with the right skills too. Many Chinese chip designers cut their teeth working for American firms like Nvidia and AMD. Kyle Chan of the Brookings Institution, a think-tank in Washington, argues that the pool is now deep enough for even small startups to recruit entire design teams.
paragraphs.zh: 中国也有不少具备正确技能的人才。许多中国芯片设计师起步时都在英伟达（Nvidia）和 AMD 等美国公司工作。华盛顿智库布鲁金斯学会（Brookings Institution）的凯尔·陈（Kyle Chan）认为，这个人才池现在已经足够深厚，甚至连小型初创企业都能招到完整的设计团队。

paragraphs.en: Talented designers are finding ways round sanctions. One approach is to use brute force. Huawei’s CloudMatrix system links 384 of the company’s Ascend processors to challenge Nvidia’s latest AI systems (though it consumes four times as much power). Another is tighter integration of hardware and software. In April DeepSeek, a Chinese AI lab, released a large language model tuned to Huawei’s silicon. Chinese designers are also embracing FP8, a lower-precision data format that helps models run efficiently on less powerful chips. Huawei is also working to undermine the advantage Nvidia enjoys from its widely used CUDA software. Its own platform, CANN, is designed to make switching from Nvidia’s chips easier. The founder of one Chinese AI startup says that the software gap, once enormous, is starting to close.
paragraphs.zh: 有才华的设计师正在寻找绕过制裁的方法。一种做法是用算力“硬碰硬”。华为的 CloudMatrix 系统将公司的 384 颗 Ascend 处理器连接起来，来挑战英伟达的最新 AI 系统，尽管其耗电量高出四倍。另一种是更紧密地把硬件和软件结合在一起。四月，中国 AI 实验室 DeepSeek 发布了一款针对华为硅片调优的大型语言模型。中国设计师还在采用 FP8，这是一种更低精度的数据格式，可让模型在算力较弱的芯片上更高效地运行。华为也在努力削弱英伟达凭借其广泛使用的 CUDA 软件而形成的优势。华为的自有平台 CANN 旨在让从英伟达芯片迁移更容易。一位中国 AI 初创企业创始人表示，这个曾经巨大软件差距正在开始缩小。

paragraphs.en: But such resourcefulness can take China only so far. Bernstein notes that Huawei’s top chip, the Ascend 910C, delivers just four-fifths of the performance of Nvidia’s H100, released almost four years ago. Homegrown chips compete mainly with the degraded processors Nvidia is allowed to sell in China (see chart 2). They are also mostly confined to “inference”—running existing models—rather than the far more demanding task of training frontier AI from scratch.
paragraphs.zh: 但这种能动性只能让中国走到一定程度。伯恩斯坦指出，华为的旗舰芯片昇腾910C的性能仅有NVIDIA四年前发布的H100的五分之四。国产芯片主要与NVIDIA在中国获准销售的降级处理器竞争（见图2）。它们也主要局限于“推理”——运行已有模型——而非更为高要求的从零开始训练前沿人工智能模型任务。

paragraphs.en: The remaining gap is less in designing chips than in making them. The fastest processors require the most advanced production lines. Chinese designers are barred from using the latest processes from foundries such as TSMC, a Taiwanese contract manufacturer. Domestic foundries, cut off from purchasing extreme-ultraviolet (EUV) lithography gear from ASML, a Dutch toolmaker, cannot reliably mass-produce chips with transistors less than seven nanometres across (the cutting edge is 3nm and below). Even for less advanced chips, capacity is constrained.
paragraphs.zh: 剩下的差距在于制造而不是设计。最快的处理器需要最先进的生产线。中国设计方被禁止使用台积电等台湾代工厂的最新制程。国内晶圆厂因被切断从荷兰设备制造商ASML购买极紫外（EUV）光刻设备的渠道，无法稳定地大规模生产晶体管小于7纳米的芯片（技术前沿已到3纳米及以下）。即使是较低阶芯片，产能也受到限制。

paragraphs.en: Consequently, China’s demand for AI processors far outstrips what it can procure. Zephyr, a pseudonymous analyst at Citrini, a research firm, notes that Chinese companies make up for the shortfall by renting cloud capacity abroad or by smuggling chips into the country. Advanced memory is another bottleneck. Almost all the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) needed for AI is made by three firms: Micron of America, and Samsung and SK Hynix of South Korea.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，中国对AI处理器的需求远远超过其可获取的数量。Citrini研究公司的一位匿名分析师Zephyr指出，中国企业通过在海外租用云算力或走私芯片来弥补这一缺口。先进存储器是另一个瓶颈。几乎所有AI所需的高带宽内存（HBM）都由三家公司生产：美国的美光，以及韩国的三星和SK海力士。

paragraphs.en: China is spending heavily to catch up. Since 2019 annual imports of chipmaking kit have tripled, to $39bn, accounting for some 40% of global demand. Much of this is to produce older chips, but some is being used for advanced fabrication. At the same time, local firms like Naura and AMEC now offer competitive alternatives for tools in critical steps like chemical deposition, etching and wafer cleaning. China is even making headway in HBMs. SemiAnalysis, a research firm, expects CXMT’s share of global HBM production to rise from almost nothing today to around 12% by 2028.
paragraphs.zh: 中国正投入巨资追赶。自2019年以来，芯片制造设备年进口量已增长到三倍，达到390亿美元，占全球需求的约40%。其中很大一部分用于生产较旧的芯片，但也有一部分用于先进工艺制造。同时，本土企业如中芯国际旗下设备供应链的Naura与AMEC正在关键工艺环节（如化学沉积、刻蚀和晶圆清洗）提供具有竞争力的替代方案。中国在HBM领域也在取得进展。研究公司SemiAnalysis预测，长鑫存储（CXMT）在全球HBM产量中的份额将从目前几乎为零提升到2028年的约12%。

paragraphs.en: For all the progress, however, China’s self-sufficiency drive hits a wall with EUV lithography, essential for printing tiny circuits. Despite years of access to ASML’s older deep-ultraviolet (DUV) machines, China has yet to produce a domestic equivalent. Most industry estimates put a Chinese-made EUV at around a decade away, though America’s commerce department believes the country may have obtained one machine illicitly.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，尽管进展可观，中国的自给自足推进在极紫外（EUV）光刻上遭遇瓶颈，而这项技术对印刷极小电路至关重要。尽管多年来可以使用阿斯麦（ASML）的旧款深紫外（DUV）光刻机，中国仍未能生产出可替代的国产EUV设备。多数行业估计认为，中国自研EUV至少还需要约十年时间，尽管美国商务部则认为中国可能已经通过非法方式获得过一台这种设备。

paragraphs.en: Without these advanced printers, Chinese chipmakers are pursuing workarounds to extract more performance from DUV machines. One is “multi-patterning”, which repeatedly exposes a single silicon wafer to multiple passes of a lithography tool. This etches smaller features, but adds cost, slows production and increases the chance of defects. Another is advanced packaging, which links multiple chips made using older technology into a single system. Huawei’s announcement before Computex signalled that the company was seeking to get closer to leading-edge performance using older DUV tools.
paragraphs.zh: 在缺少这些先进设备的情况下，中国芯片制造商正在采取替代方案，以期从DUV机台中榨取更高性能。一种方法是“多重图形化”，即反复让单片硅晶圆经过同一套光刻工具的多次曝光。这样可以刻出更小的结构，但会提高成本、放慢产能并增加缺陷风险。另一种方法是先进封装工艺，把多颗采用较旧工艺制造的芯片连接成一个整体系统。华为在Computex之前发布的公告表明，公司正在努力借助更早代际的DUV设备接近最先进的性能水平。

paragraphs.en: Although China is still some distance from chipmaking’s technological frontier, its progress should not be ignored. Consider Apple. Amid a worsening shortage of memory chips, the iPhone-maker is seeking permission from the American government to buy older-generation memory from CXMT. If it wins approval, an American business will be helping to bankroll the very industry its government has spent years trying to contain. ■
paragraphs.zh: 尽管中国在芯片制造的技术前沿上仍有一段距离，但其进展不应被忽视。以苹果公司为例。随着内存芯片短缺加剧，这家iPhone制造商正在寻求美国政府许可，以购买由长鑫存储（CXMT）提供的旧一代内存芯片。如果其获得批准，美国企业就将为其政府多年来试图遏制的产业提供资金支持。■

paragraphs.en: To track the trends shaping commerce, industry and technology, sign up to “The Bottom Line”, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter on global business.
paragraphs.zh: 想要追踪正在重塑商业、产业和技术格局的趋势，请订阅《The Bottom Line》，我们每周仅对订阅者开放的全球商业通讯。
